summary: Scorching Hard To Handle and iconic Alligator jam, tight second set
blurb: The show bursts open with a blistering Hard To Handle and rides a soaring Dark Hollow, while the second set delivers an all‑out Alligator > Cold Rain & Snow marathon that rolls into China Cat Sunflower. Jerry’s lead work is repeatedly called incredible, Pigpen’s organ shines, and Phil lays down a pure groove throughout.
review: On April 29 1971 at the Fillmore East the Dead served up one of their most electrifying late‑spring nights. The first set built momentum with a scorching Hard To Handle, a crisp Dark Hollow, and a soulful Bird Song that hinted at the fireworks to come. The second set was a masterclass in improvisation: an explosive Alligator jam segued seamlessly into Cold Rain And Snow before launching into a joyous China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider pairing, all anchored by Pigpen’s organ brilliance and Phil’s rock‑solid bass foundation. Jerry Garcia’s guitar work earned descriptors like “incredible” and “turn‑out tone,” while Bob Weir’s rhythm chops kept the groove tight. The night closed with a rousing encore that left the audience buzzing, cementing this performance as a historic high point of the Fillmore East run.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across twelve recordings the overwhelming tone is positive: multiple reviewers call the performance 'scorching', 'iconic' and 'legendary,' repeatedly praising Hard To Handle, Alligator > Cold Rain & Snow, Bird Song, Dark Hollow and Pigpen's organ. Only a few mixed or negative notes focus on recording quality or isolated set‑list spots, not the overall musicianship, yielding a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Outstanding multitrack soundboard capture with crisp, deep mix; organ and guitar are especially audible, delivering the most vivid listening experience of this night.

key_highlights: Hard To Handle launches the show with blistering energy.
Alligator > Cold Rain & Snow jam is a centerpiece of improvisational fire.
Pigpen’s organ shines throughout, rare clarity for a 1971 SBD.
Jerry Garcia’s lead work described as “incredible” on multiple tracks.

band_performance.Jerry: His lead guitar is repeatedly called incredible, with a razor‑sharp tone on Hard To Handle and soaring solos on Bird Song and Dark Hollow; his overall playing is described as inspiring and solid throughout the night.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a pure groove and deep foundation; reviewers note “bombs” during jams and call his bass work five‑star material for this show.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers strong rhythm and vocal presence, slashing through songs like Hard To Handle and adding charismatic Pigpen‑style organ flair on several numbers.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s drum solo between Alligator and the jam is highlighted as “in your living room,” and his playing overall adds power to the second‑set peaks.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
